title: (BUG) cli: received file saved to CWD, not data-dir
description: In commands.rs, when a FileChunk transfer completes, the assembled file is written via `tokio::fs::write(&safe, &assembled)` where `safe` is just a filename (no directory prefix). This writes to the process's current working directory, not the data directory or any user-configured downloads folder. A remote peer can name a file 'bashrc' or 'authorized_keys' and — if the process happens to have been started from $HOME or $HOME/.ssh — overwrite those files. Even without that race, the file location is surprising and undocumented. Fix: prefix the path with a configured downloads directory (e.g., data_dir.join('downloads').join(&safe)) and create the directory at startup. Lines 1270 and 1500 in cli/src/commands.rs.
